 24→class SettingsExporter(
 25→    private val contentResolver: ContentResolver,
 26→    private val preferences: Preferences,
 27→    private val folderSettingsProvider: FolderSettingsProvider,
 28→    private val folderRepository: FolderRepository,
 29→    private val notificationSettingsUpdater: NotificationSettingsUpdater,
 30→) {
 31→    @Throws(SettingsImportExportException::class)
 32→    fun exportToUri(includeGlobals: Boolean, accountUuids: Set<String>, uri: Uri) {
 33→        try {
 34→            contentResolver.openOutputStream(uri, "wt")!!.use { outputStream ->
 35→                exportPreferences(outputStream, includeGlobals, accountUuids, includePasswords = false)
 36→            }
 37→        } catch (e: Exception) {
 38→            throw SettingsImportExportException(e)
 39→        }
 40→    }
 41→
 42→    @Throws(SettingsImportExportException::class)
 43→    fun exportPreferences(
 44→        outputStream: OutputStream,
 45→        includeGlobals: Boolean,
 46→        accountUuids: Set<String>,
 47→        includePasswords: Boolean,
 48→    ) {
 49→        updateNotificationSettings(accountUuids)
 50→
 51→        try {
 52→            val serializer = Xml.newSerializer()
 53→            serializer.setOutput(outputStream, "UTF-8")
 54→
 55→            serializer.startDocument(null, true)
 56→
 57→            // Output with indentation
 58→            serializer.setFeature("http://xmlpull.org/v1/doc/features.html#indent-output", true)
 59→
 60→            serializer.startTag(null, ROOT_ELEMENT)
 61→            serializer.attribute(null, VERSION_ATTRIBUTE, Settings.VERSION.toString())
 62→            serializer.attribute(null, FILE_FORMAT_ATTRIBUTE, FILE_FORMAT_VERSION.toString())
 63→
 64→            Timber.i("Exporting preferences")
 65→
 66→            val storage = preferences.storage
 67→
 68→            val prefs: Map<String, Any> = storage.all.toSortedMap()
 69→            if (includeGlobals) {
 70→                serializer.startTag(null, GLOBAL_ELEMENT)
 71→                writeSettings(serializer, prefs)
 72→                serializer.endTag(null, GLOBAL_ELEMENT)
 73→            }
 74→
 75→            serializer.startTag(null, ACCOUNTS_ELEMENT)
 76→            for (accountUuid in accountUuids) {
 77→                preferences.getAccount(accountUuid)?.let { account ->
 78→                    writeAccount(serializer, account, prefs, includePasswords)
 79→                }
 80→            }
 81→            serializer.endTag(null, ACCOUNTS_ELEMENT)
 82→
 83→            serializer.endTag(null, ROOT_ELEMENT)
 84→            serializer.endDocument()
 85→            serializer.flush()
 86→        } catch (e: Exception) {
 87→            throw SettingsImportExportException(e.localizedMessage, e)
 88→        }
 89→    }

 91→    private fun updateNotificationSettings(accountUuids: Set<String>) {
 92→        try {
 93→            notificationSettingsUpdater.updateNotificationSettings(accountUuids)
 94→        } catch (e: Exception) {
 95→            // An error here could mean we export notification settings that don't reflect the current configuration
 96→            // of the notification channels. But we prefer stale data over failing the export.
 97→            Timber.w(e, "Error while updating accounts with notification configuration from system")
 98→        }
 99→    }

101→    private fun writeSettings(serializer: XmlSerializer, prefs: Map<String, Any>) {
102→        for ((key, versions) in GeneralSettingsDescriptions.SETTINGS) {
103→            val valueString = prefs[key] as String?
104→            val highestVersion = versions.lastKey()
105→            val setting = versions[highestVersion] ?: continue // Setting was removed
106→
107→            if (valueString != null) {
108→                try {
109→                    writeKeyAndPrettyValueFromSetting(serializer, key, setting, valueString)
110→                } catch (e: InvalidSettingValueException) {
111→                    Timber.w(
112→                        "Global setting \"%s\" has invalid value \"%s\" in preference storage. This shouldn't happen!",
113→                        key,
114→                        valueString,
115→                    )
116→                }
117→            } else {
118→                Timber.d("Couldn't find key \"%s\" in preference storage. Using default value.", key)
119→                writeKeyAndDefaultValueFromSetting(serializer, key, setting)
120→            }
121→        }
122→    }

124→    private fun writeAccount(
125→        serializer: XmlSerializer,
126→        account: Account,
127→        prefs: Map<String, Any>,
128→        includePasswords: Boolean,
129→    ) {
130→        val identities = mutableSetOf<Int>()
131→        val accountUuid = account.uuid
132→
133→        serializer.startTag(null, ACCOUNT_ELEMENT)
134→        serializer.attribute(null, UUID_ATTRIBUTE, accountUuid)
135→
136→        val name = prefs["$accountUuid.$ACCOUNT_DESCRIPTION_KEY"] as String?
137→        if (name != null) {
138→            serializer.startTag(null, NAME_ELEMENT)
139→            serializer.text(name)
140→            serializer.endTag(null, NAME_ELEMENT)
141→        }
142→
143→        // Write incoming server settings
144→        val incoming = account.incomingServerSettings
145→        serializer.startTag(null, INCOMING_SERVER_ELEMENT)
146→        serializer.attribute(null, TYPE_ATTRIBUTE, fromServerSettingsType(incoming.type))
147→
148→        writeElement(serializer, HOST_ELEMENT, incoming.host)
149→        if (incoming.port != -1) {
150→            writeElement(serializer, PORT_ELEMENT, incoming.port.toString())
151→        }
152→        writeElement(serializer, CONNECTION_SECURITY_ELEMENT, incoming.connectionSecurity.name)
153→        writeElement(serializer, AUTHENTICATION_TYPE_ELEMENT, incoming.authenticationType.name)
154→        writeElement(serializer, USERNAME_ELEMENT, incoming.username)
155→        writeElement(serializer, CLIENT_CERTIFICATE_ALIAS_ELEMENT, incoming.clientCertificateAlias)
156→
157→        if (includePasswords && !incoming.password.isNullOrEmpty()) {
158→            writeElement(serializer, PASSWORD_ELEMENT, incoming.password)
159→        }
160→
161→        var extras = incoming.extra
162→        if (!extras.isNullOrEmpty()) {
163→            serializer.startTag(null, EXTRA_ELEMENT)
164→            for ((key, value) in extras) {
165→                writeKeyAndPrettyValueFromSetting(serializer, key, value)
166→            }
167→            serializer.endTag(null, EXTRA_ELEMENT)
168→        }
169→        serializer.endTag(null, INCOMING_SERVER_ELEMENT)
170→
171→        // Write outgoing server settings
172→        val outgoing = account.outgoingServerSettings
173→        serializer.startTag(null, OUTGOING_SERVER_ELEMENT)
174→        serializer.attribute(null, TYPE_ATTRIBUTE, fromServerSettingsType(outgoing.type))
175→
176→        writeElement(serializer, HOST_ELEMENT, outgoing.host)
177→        if (outgoing.port != -1) {
178→            writeElement(serializer, PORT_ELEMENT, outgoing.port.toString())
179→        }
180→        writeElement(serializer, CONNECTION_SECURITY_ELEMENT, outgoing.connectionSecurity.name)
181→        writeElement(serializer, AUTHENTICATION_TYPE_ELEMENT, outgoing.authenticationType.name)
182→        writeElement(serializer, USERNAME_ELEMENT, outgoing.username)
183→        writeElement(serializer, CLIENT_CERTIFICATE_ALIAS_ELEMENT, outgoing.clientCertificateAlias)
184→
185→        if (includePasswords && !outgoing.password.isNullOrEmpty()) {
186→            writeElement(serializer, PASSWORD_ELEMENT, outgoing.password)
187→        }
188→
189→        extras = outgoing.extra
190→        if (!extras.isNullOrEmpty()) {
191→            serializer.startTag(null, EXTRA_ELEMENT)
192→            for ((key, value) in extras) {
193→                writeKeyAndPrettyValueFromSetting(serializer, key, value)
194→            }
195→            serializer.endTag(null, EXTRA_ELEMENT)
196→        }
197→        serializer.endTag(null, OUTGOING_SERVER_ELEMENT)
198→
199→        // Write account settings
200→        serializer.startTag(null, SETTINGS_ELEMENT)
201→        for ((key, value) in prefs) {
202→            val valueString = value.toString()
203→            val comps = key.split(".", limit = 2)
204→
205→            if (comps.size < 2) {
206→                // Skip global settings
207→                continue
208→            }
209→
210→            val keyUuid = comps[0]
211→            val keyPart = comps[1]
212→
213→            if (keyUuid != accountUuid) {
214→                // Setting doesn't belong to the account we're currently writing.
215→                continue
216→            }
217→
218→            val indexOfLastDot = keyPart.lastIndexOf(".")
219→            val hasThirdPart = indexOfLastDot != -1 && indexOfLastDot < keyPart.length - 1
220→            if (hasThirdPart) {
221→                val secondPart = keyPart.substring(0, indexOfLastDot)
222→                val thirdPart = keyPart.substring(indexOfLastDot + 1)
223→                if (secondPart == IDENTITY_EMAIL_KEY) {
224→                    // This is an identity key. Save identity index for later...
225→                    thirdPart.toIntOrNull()?.let {
226→                        identities.add(it)
227→                    }
228→                    // ... but don't write it now.
229→                    continue
230→                }
231→
232→                if (FolderSettingsDescriptions.SETTINGS.containsKey(thirdPart)) {
233→                    // This is a folder key. Ignore it.
234→                    continue
235→                }
236→            }
237→
238→            if (keyPart !in FOLDER_NAME_KEYS) {
239→                writeAccountSettingIfValid(serializer, keyPart, valueString, account)
240→            }
241→        }
242→
243→        writeFolderNameSettings(account, folderRepository, serializer)
244→
245→        serializer.endTag(null, SETTINGS_ELEMENT)
246→
247→        if (identities.isNotEmpty()) {
248→            serializer.startTag(null, IDENTITIES_ELEMENT)
249→
250→            // Sort identity indices (that's why we store them as Integers)
251→            val sortedIdentities = identities.sorted()
252→            for (identityIndex in sortedIdentities) {
253→                writeIdentity(serializer, accountUuid, identityIndex.toString(), prefs)
254→            }
255→            serializer.endTag(null, IDENTITIES_ELEMENT)
256→        }
257→
258→        val folders = folderSettingsProvider.getFolderSettings(account)
259→        if (folders.isNotEmpty()) {
260→            serializer.startTag(null, FOLDERS_ELEMENT)
261→            for (folder in folders) {
262→                writeFolder(serializer, folder)
263→            }
264→            serializer.endTag(null, FOLDERS_ELEMENT)
265→        }
266→
267→        serializer.endTag(null, ACCOUNT_ELEMENT)
268→    }

270→    private fun writeAccountSettingIfValid(
271→        serializer: XmlSerializer,
272→        keyPart: String,
273→        valueString: String,
274→        account: Account,
275→    ) {
276→        val versionedSetting = AccountSettingsDescriptions.SETTINGS[keyPart]
277→        if (versionedSetting != null) {
278→            val highestVersion = versionedSetting.lastKey()
279→
280→            val setting = versionedSetting[highestVersion]
281→            if (setting != null) {
282→                // Only export account settings that can be found in AccountSettings.SETTINGS
283→                try {
284→                    writeKeyAndPrettyValueFromSetting(serializer, keyPart, setting, valueString)
285→                } catch (e: InvalidSettingValueException) {
286→                    Timber.w(
287→                        "Account setting \"%s\" (%s) has invalid value \"%s\" in preference storage. " +
288→                            "This shouldn't happen!",
289→                        keyPart,
290→                        account,
291→                        valueString,
292→                    )
293→                }
294→            }
295→        }
296→    }

298→    private fun writeFolderNameSettings(
299→        account: Account,
300→        folderRepository: FolderRepository,
301→        serializer: XmlSerializer,
302→    ) {
303→        fun writeFolderNameSetting(
304→            key: String,
305→            folderId: Long?,
306→            importedFolderServerId: String?,
307→            writeEmptyValue: Boolean = false,
308→        ) {
309→            val folderServerId = folderId?.let {
310→                folderRepository.getFolderServerId(account, folderId)
311→            } ?: importedFolderServerId
312→
313→            if (folderServerId != null) {
314→                writeAccountSettingIfValid(serializer, key, folderServerId, account)
315→            } else if (writeEmptyValue) {
316→                writeAccountSettingIfValid(serializer, key, valueString = "", account)
317→            }
318→        }
319→
320→        writeFolderNameSetting(
321→            "autoExpandFolderName",
322→            account.autoExpandFolderId,
323→            account.importedAutoExpandFolder,
324→            writeEmptyValue = true,
325→        )
326→        writeFolderNameSetting("archiveFolderName", account.archiveFolderId, account.importedArchiveFolder)
327→        writeFolderNameSetting("draftsFolderName", account.draftsFolderId, account.importedDraftsFolder)
328→        writeFolderNameSetting("sentFolderName", account.sentFolderId, account.importedSentFolder)
329→        writeFolderNameSetting("spamFolderName", account.spamFolderId, account.importedSpamFolder)
330→        writeFolderNameSetting("trashFolderName", account.trashFolderId, account.importedTrashFolder)
331→    }

333→    private fun writeIdentity(
334→        serializer: XmlSerializer,
335→        accountUuid: String,
336→        identity: String,
337→        prefs: Map<String, Any>,
338→    ) {
339→        serializer.startTag(null, IDENTITY_ELEMENT)
340→
341→        val prefix = "$accountUuid."
342→        val suffix = ".$identity"
343→
344→        // Write name belonging to the identity
345→        val name = prefs[prefix + IDENTITY_NAME_KEY + suffix] as String?
346→        if (name != null) {
347→            serializer.startTag(null, NAME_ELEMENT)
348→            serializer.text(name)
349→            serializer.endTag(null, NAME_ELEMENT)
350→        }
351→
352→        // Write email address belonging to the identity
353→        val email = prefs[prefix + IDENTITY_EMAIL_KEY + suffix] as String?
354→        serializer.startTag(null, EMAIL_ELEMENT)
355→        serializer.text(email)
356→        serializer.endTag(null, EMAIL_ELEMENT)
357→
358→        // Write identity description
359→        val description = prefs[prefix + IDENTITY_DESCRIPTION_KEY + suffix] as String?
360→        if (description != null) {
361→            serializer.startTag(null, DESCRIPTION_ELEMENT)
362→            serializer.text(description)
363→            serializer.endTag(null, DESCRIPTION_ELEMENT)
364→        }
365→
366→        // Write identity settings
367→        serializer.startTag(null, SETTINGS_ELEMENT)
368→        for ((key, value) in prefs) {
369→            val valueString = value.toString()
370→
371→            val comps = key.split(".")
372→            if (comps.size < 3) {
373→                // Skip non-identity config entries
374→                continue
375→            }
376→
377→            val keyUuid = comps[0]
378→            val identityKey = comps[1]
379→            val identityIndex = comps[2]
380→            if (keyUuid != accountUuid || identityIndex != identity) {
381→                // Skip entries that belong to another identity
382→                continue
383→            }
384→
385→            val versionedSetting = IdentitySettingsDescriptions.SETTINGS[identityKey]
386→            if (versionedSetting != null) {
387→                val highestVersion = versionedSetting.lastKey()
388→                val setting = versionedSetting[highestVersion]
389→                if (setting != null) {
390→                    // Only write settings that have an entry in IdentitySettings.SETTINGS
391→                    try {
392→                        writeKeyAndPrettyValueFromSetting(serializer, identityKey, setting, valueString)
393→                    } catch (e: InvalidSettingValueException) {
394→                        Timber.w(
395→                            "Identity setting \"%s\" has invalid value \"%s\" in preference storage. " +
396→                                "This shouldn't happen!",
397→                            identityKey,
398→                            valueString,
399→                        )
400→                    }
401→                }
402→            }
403→        }
404→        serializer.endTag(null, SETTINGS_ELEMENT)
405→
406→        serializer.endTag(null, IDENTITY_ELEMENT)
407→    }

 7→
 8→internal class GeneralSettingsWriter(
 9→    private val preferences: Preferences,
10→    private val generalSettingsManager: RealGeneralSettingsManager,
11→) {
12→    fun write(settings: InternalSettingsMap): Boolean {
13→        // Convert general settings to the string representation used in preference storage
14→        val stringSettings = GeneralSettingsDescriptions.convert(settings)
15→
16→        val editor = preferences.createStorageEditor()
17→
18→        // Use current general settings as base and overwrite with validated settings read from the import file.
19→        val mergedSettings = GeneralSettingsDescriptions.getGlobalSettings(preferences.storage).toMutableMap()
20→        mergedSettings.putAll(stringSettings)
21→
22→        for ((key, value) in mergedSettings) {
23→            editor.putStringWithLogging(key, value)
24→        }
25→
26→        return if (editor.commit()) {
27→            Timber.v("Committed general settings to the preference storage.")
28→
29→            generalSettingsManager.loadSettings()
30→
31→            true
32→        } else {
33→            Timber.v("Failed to commit general settings to the preference storage")
34→            false
35→        }
36→    }
37→}
38→
39→/**
40→ * Write to a [StorageEditor] while logging what is written if debug logging is enabled.
41→ */
42→internal fun StorageEditor.putStringWithLogging(key: String, value: String?) {
43→    if (K9.isDebugLoggingEnabled) {
44→        var outputValue = value
45→        if (!K9.isSensitiveDebugLoggingEnabled &&
46→            (
47→                key.endsWith("." + AccountPreferenceSerializer.OUTGOING_SERVER_SETTINGS_KEY) ||
48→                    key.endsWith("." + AccountPreferenceSerializer.INCOMING_SERVER_SETTINGS_KEY)
49→                )
50→        ) {
51→            outputValue = "*sensitive*"
52→        }
53→
54→        Timber.v("Setting %s=%s", key, outputValue)
55→    }
56→
57→    putString(key, value)
58→}

 22→/**
 23→ * A `ContentProvider` that makes settings available to another app.
 24→ *
 25→ * This can be used when migrating from one app to another, e.g. from K-9 Mail to Thunderbird for Android.
 26→ * Only apps on the allowlist (see [isTrustedCaller()][SettingsProvider.isTrustedCaller]) are allowed access to the
 27→ * settings (including passwords).
 28→ */
 29→class SettingsProvider : ContentProvider(), KoinComponent {
 30→    private val accountManager: AccountManager by inject()
 31→    private val settingsExporter: SettingsExporter by inject()
 32→
 33→    override fun onCreate(): Boolean {
 34→        return true
 35→    }
 36→
 37→    override fun getType(uri: Uri): String {
 38→        return MimeTypeUtil.K9_SETTINGS_MIME_TYPE
 39→    }
 40→
 41→    override fun openFile(uri: Uri, mode: String): ParcelFileDescriptor? {
 42→        if (!isTrustedCaller()) {
 43→            Timber.d("Caller must be in the allowlist")
 44→            return null
 45→        }
 46→
 47→        val (readFileDescriptor, writeFileDescriptor) = ParcelFileDescriptor.createPipe()
 48→
 49→        thread {
 50→            val accountUuids = accountManager.getAccounts().mapToSet { it.uuid }
 51→            ParcelFileDescriptor.AutoCloseOutputStream(writeFileDescriptor).use { outputStream ->
 52→                settingsExporter.exportPreferences(
 53→                    outputStream,
 54→                    includeGlobals = true,
 55→                    accountUuids,
 56→                    includePasswords = true,
 57→                )
 58→            }
 59→        }
 60→
 61→        return readFileDescriptor
 62→    }

 64→    override fun insert(uri: Uri, values: ContentValues?): Uri? {
 65→        throw UnsupportedOperationException("not implemented")
 66→    }
 67→
 68→    override fun query(
 69→        uri: Uri,
 70→        projection: Array<out String>?,
 71→        selection: String?,
 72→        selectionArgs: Array<out String>?,
 73→        sortOrder: String?,
 74→    ): Cursor? {
 75→        throw UnsupportedOperationException("not implemented")
 76→    }
 77→

 79→        throw UnsupportedOperationException("not implemented")
 80→    }
 81→
 82→    override fun delete(uri: Uri, selection: String?, selectionArgs: Array<out String>?): Int {
 83→        throw UnsupportedOperationException("not implemented")
 84→    }
 85→
 86→    /**
 87→     * A trusted caller must appear exactly in our allowlist map - its package map must map to a known signature.
 88→     * In case of any deviation (multiple signers, certificate rotation), assume that the caller isn't trusted.
 89→     */
 90→    // Based on https://searchfox.org/mozilla-esr68/source/mobile/android/services/src/main/java/org/mozilla/gecko/fxa/AuthStateProvider.java
 91→    @Suppress("ReturnCount")
 92→    private fun isTrustedCaller(): Boolean {
 93→        val packageManager = context!!.packageManager
 94→
 95→        // Signatures can be easily obtained locally. For an APK in question, unzip it and run:
 96→        // keytool -printcert -file META-INF/SIGNATURE.RSA
 97→        // SHA256 certificate fingerprint is what's listed below.
 98→
 99→        // We will only service query requests from callers that exactly match our allowlist.
100→        // Allowlist is local to this function to avoid exposing it to the world more than necessary.
101→        val packageAllowlist = listOf(
102→            // K-9 Mail (our signing key)
103→            "com.fsck.k9" to "55c8a523b97335f5bf60dfe8a9f3e1dde744516d9357e80a925b7b22e4f55524",
104→            // K-9 Mail (F-Droid)
105→            "com.fsck.k9" to "c430665e3662253b2078dcda350c2c6ce44d915a3d8a147b63ced619bb9e8576",
106→            // Thunderbird for Android (release)
107→            "net.thunderbird.android" to "b6524779b3dbbc5ac17a5ac271ddb29dcfbf723578c238e03c3c217811356dd1",
108→            // Thunderbird for Android (beta)
109→            "net.thunderbird.android.beta" to "056bfafb450249502fd9226228704c2529e1b822da06760d47a85c9557741fbd",
110→            // Thunderbird for Android (daily)
111→            "net.thunderbird.android.daily" to "c48d74a75c45cd362b0ff2c1e9756f541dee816163e3684a9fd59f6c3ae949b2",
112→        )
113→
114→        val callerPackage = callingPackage ?: return false
115→        val expectedHashes = packageAllowlist
116→            .asSequence()
117→            .filter { it.first == callerPackage }
118→            .map { it.second }
119→            .toList()
120→            .takeIf { it.isNotEmpty() }
121→            ?: return false
122→
123→        val callerSignature = if (Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= Build.VERSION_CODES.P) {
124→            getSignaturePostApi28(packageManager, callerPackage)
125→        } else {
126→            getSignaturePreApi28(packageManager, callerPackage)
127→        }
128→
129→        if (callerSignature == null) {
130→            Timber.v("Couldn't retrieve caller signature")
131→            return false
132→        }
133→
134→        val callerSignatureHash = callerSignature.toByteArray().toByteString().sha256().hex()
135→        val result = callerSignatureHash in expectedHashes
136→        if (result) {
137→            Timber.d("Caller %s signature fingerprint matches %s", callerPackage, callerSignatureHash)
138→        } else {
139→            Timber.d("Failed! Signature mismatch for calling package %s (%s)", callerPackage, callerSignatureHash)
140→        }
141→
142→        return result
143→    }
144→
145→    @Suppress("DEPRECATION")
146→    private fun getSignaturePreApi28(packageManager: PackageManager, callerPackage: String): Signature? {
147→        // For older APIs, we use the deprecated `signatures` field, which isn't aware of certificate rotation.
148→        val packageInfo = packageManager.getPackageInfo(callerPackage, PackageManager.GET_SIGNATURES)
149→
150→        // We don't expect our callers to have multiple signers, so we don't service such requests.
151→        if (packageInfo.signatures.size != 1) {
152→            return null
153→        }
154→
155→        // In case of signature rotation, this will report the oldest used certificate, pretending that the signature
156→        // rotation never took place. We can only rely on our allowlist being up-to-date in this case.
157→        return packageInfo.signatures[0]
158→    }
159→
160→    @TargetApi(Build.VERSION_CODES.P)
161→    @Suppress("ReturnCount")
162→    private fun getSignaturePostApi28(packageManager: PackageManager, callerPackage: String): Signature? {
163→        // For API28+, we can perform some extra checks.
164→        val packageInfo = packageManager.getPackageInfo(callerPackage, PackageManager.GET_SIGNING_CERTIFICATES)
165→
166→        // We don't expect our callers to have multiple signers, so we don't service such requests.
167→        if (packageInfo.signingInfo.hasMultipleSigners()) {
168→            return null
169→        }
170→
171→        // We currently don't support servicing requests from callers that performed certificate rotation.
172→        if (packageInfo.signingInfo.hasPastSigningCertificates()) {
173→            return null
174→        }
175→
176→        return packageInfo.signingInfo.signingCertificateHistory[0]
177→    }
178→}

  9→import java.io.OutputStream
 10→import org.xmlpull.v1.XmlSerializer
 11→
 12→// TODO: This duplicates much of the code in SettingsExporter. Add an abstraction layer for the input data, so we can
 13→//  use a single XML writer class for exporting accounts and writing QR code payloads to a settings file.
 14→internal class XmlSettingWriter(
 15→    private val uuidGenerator: UuidGenerator,
 16→) {
 17→    fun writeSettings(outputStream: OutputStream, accounts: List<Account>) {
 18→        val serializer = Xml.newSerializer()
 19→        serializer.setOutput(outputStream, "UTF-8")
 20→
 21→        serializer.startDocument(null, true)
 22→
 23→        // Output with indentation
 24→        serializer.setFeature("http://xmlpull.org/v1/doc/features.html#indent-output", true)
 25→
 26→        serializer.writeRoot(accounts)
 27→
 28→        serializer.endDocument()
 29→        serializer.flush()
 30→    }
 31→
 32→    private fun XmlSerializer.writeRoot(accounts: List<Account>) {
 33→        startTag(null, ROOT_ELEMENT)
 34→        attribute(null, VERSION_ATTRIBUTE, SETTINGS_VERSION.toString())
 35→        attribute(null, FILE_FORMAT_ATTRIBUTE, FILE_FORMAT_VERSION.toString())
 36→
 37→        writeAccounts(accounts)
 38→
 39→        endTag(null, ROOT_ELEMENT)
 40→    }
 41→
 42→    private fun XmlSerializer.writeAccounts(accounts: List<Account>) {
 43→        startTag(null, ACCOUNTS_ELEMENT)
 44→
 45→        for (account in accounts) {
 46→            writeAccount(account)
 47→        }
 48→
 49→        endTag(null, ACCOUNTS_ELEMENT)
 50→    }
 51→
 52→    private fun XmlSerializer.writeAccount(account: Account) {
 53→        val accountUuid = uuidGenerator.generateUuid()
 54→
 55→        startTag(null, ACCOUNT_ELEMENT)
 56→        attribute(null, UUID_ATTRIBUTE, accountUuid)
 57→
 58→        writeElement(NAME_ELEMENT, account.accountName)
 59→        writeIncomingServer(account.incomingServer)
 60→        writeOutgoingServers(account.outgoingServerGroups)
 61→
 62→        endTag(null, ACCOUNT_ELEMENT)
 63→    }
 64→
 65→    private fun XmlSerializer.writeIncomingServer(incomingServer: IncomingServer) {
 66→        startTag(null, INCOMING_SERVER_ELEMENT)
 67→        attribute(null, TYPE_ATTRIBUTE, incomingServer.protocol.mapToSettingsString())
 68→
 69→        writeElement(HOST_ELEMENT, incomingServer.hostname.value)
 70→        writeElement(PORT_ELEMENT, incomingServer.port.value.toString())
 71→        writeElement(CONNECTION_SECURITY_ELEMENT, incomingServer.connectionSecurity.mapToSettingsString())
 72→        writeElement(AUTHENTICATION_TYPE_ELEMENT, incomingServer.authenticationType.mapToSettingsString())
 73→        writeElement(USERNAME_ELEMENT, incomingServer.username)
 74→
 75→        incomingServer.password?.let { password ->
 76→            writeElement(PASSWORD_ELEMENT, password)
 77→        }
 78→
 79→        endTag(null, INCOMING_SERVER_ELEMENT)
 80→    }
 81→
 82→    private fun XmlSerializer.writeOutgoingServers(outgoingServerGroups: List<OutgoingServerGroup>) {
 83→        val outgoingServerGroup = outgoingServerGroups.first()
 84→        writeOutgoingServer(outgoingServerGroup.outgoingServer)
 85→        writeIdentities(outgoingServerGroup.identities)
 86→    }
 87→
 88→    private fun XmlSerializer.writeOutgoingServer(outgoingServer: OutgoingServer) {
 89→        startTag(null, OUTGOING_SERVER_ELEMENT)
 90→        attribute(null, TYPE_ATTRIBUTE, outgoingServer.protocol.mapToSettingsString())
 91→
 92→        writeElement(HOST_ELEMENT, outgoingServer.hostname.value)
 93→        writeElement(PORT_ELEMENT, outgoingServer.port.value.toString())
 94→        writeElement(CONNECTION_SECURITY_ELEMENT, outgoingServer.connectionSecurity.mapToSettingsString())
 95→        writeElement(AUTHENTICATION_TYPE_ELEMENT, outgoingServer.authenticationType.mapToSettingsString())
 96→        writeElement(USERNAME_ELEMENT, outgoingServer.username)
 97→
 98→        outgoingServer.password?.let { password ->
 99→            writeElement(PASSWORD_ELEMENT, password)
100→        }
101→
102→        endTag(null, OUTGOING_SERVER_ELEMENT)
103→    }
104→
105→    private fun XmlSerializer.writeIdentities(identities: List<Identity>) {
106→        startTag(null, IDENTITIES_ELEMENT)
107→
108→        for (identity in identities) {
109→            writeIdentity(identity)
110→        }
111→
112→        endTag(null, IDENTITIES_ELEMENT)
113→    }
114→
115→    private fun XmlSerializer.writeIdentity(identity: Identity) {
116→        startTag(null, IDENTITY_ELEMENT)
117→
118→        writeElement(NAME_ELEMENT, identity.displayName)
119→        writeElement(EMAIL_ELEMENT, identity.emailAddress.address)
120→
121→        endTag(null, IDENTITY_ELEMENT)
122→    }
123→
124→    private fun XmlSerializer.writeElement(elementName: String, value: String) {
125→        startTag(null, elementName)
126→        text(value)
127→        endTag(null, elementName)
128→    }
129→

131→        // It's not necessary to keep this in sync with com.fsck.k9.preferences.Settings.VERSION because the import
132→        // code supports importing old settings files.

134→        private const val FILE_FORMAT_VERSION = 1
135→

149→        private const val USERNAME_ELEMENT = "username"

16→    fun createSpecialLocalFolders(account: Account) {
17→        Timber.d("Creating special local folders")
18→
19→        val localStore = localStoreProvider.getInstance(account)
20→
21→        if (account.outboxFolderId == null) {
22→            account.outboxFolderId = localStore.createLocalFolder(OUTBOX_FOLDER_NAME, FolderType.OUTBOX)
23→        } else {
24→            Timber.d("Outbox folder was already set up")
25→        }
26→
27→        if (account.isPop3()) {
28→            if (account.draftsFolderId == null) {
29→                val draftsFolderId = localStore.createLocalFolder(DRAFTS_FOLDER_NAME, FolderType.DRAFTS)
30→                account.setDraftsFolderId(draftsFolderId, SpecialFolderSelection.MANUAL)
31→            } else {
32→                Timber.d("Drafts folder was already set up")
33→            }
34→
35→            if (account.sentFolderId == null) {
36→                val sentFolderId = localStore.createLocalFolder(SENT_FOLDER_NAME, FolderType.SENT)
37→                account.setSentFolderId(sentFolderId, SpecialFolderSelection.MANUAL)
38→            } else {
39→                Timber.d("Sent folder was already set up")
40→            }
41→
42→            if (account.trashFolderId == null) {
43→                val trashFolderId = localStore.createLocalFolder(TRASH_FOLDER_NAME, FolderType.TRASH)
44→                account.setTrashFolderId(trashFolderId, SpecialFolderSelection.MANUAL)
45→            } else {
46→                Timber.d("Trash folder was already set up")
47→            }
48→        }
49→
50→        preferences.saveAccount(account)
51→    }
52→
53→    fun createOutbox(account: Account): Long {
54→        Timber.d("Creating Outbox folder")
55→
56→        val localStore = localStoreProvider.getInstance(account)
57→        val outboxFolderId = localStore.createLocalFolder(OUTBOX_FOLDER_NAME, FolderType.OUTBOX)
58→
59→        account.outboxFolderId = outboxFolderId
60→        preferences.saveAccount(account)
61→
62→        return outboxFolderId
63→    }

1→package com.fsck.k9.preferences
 2→
 3→import com.fsck.k9.K9
 4→import com.fsck.k9.preferences.Settings.SettingsDescription
 5→import timber.log.Timber
 6→
 7→internal object SettingsUpgradeHelper {
 8→    /**
 9→     * Upgrade settings using the settings structure and/or special upgrade code.
10→     *
11→     * @param version
12→     *   The content version of the settings in `settings`.
13→     * @param upgraders
14→     *   A map of [SettingsUpgrader]s for nontrivial settings upgrades.
15→     * @param settingsDescriptions
16→     *   The structure describing the different settings, possibly containing multiple versions.
17→     * @param settings
18→     *   The validated settings as returned by [Settings.validate].
19→     *
20→     * @return The upgraded settings.
21→     */
22→    @JvmStatic
23→    fun upgrade(
24→        version: Int,
25→        upgraders: Map<Int, SettingsUpgrader>,
26→        settingsDescriptions: SettingsDescriptions,
27→        settings: Map<String, Any?>,
28→    ): Map<String, Any?> {
29→        return upgradeToVersion(Settings.VERSION, version, upgraders, settingsDescriptions, settings)
30→    }
31→
32→    fun upgradeToVersion(
33→        targetVersion: Int,
34→        version: Int,
35→        upgraders: Map<Int, SettingsUpgrader>,
36→        settingsDescriptions: SettingsDescriptions,
37→        settings: Map<String, Any?>,
38→    ): Map<String, Any?> {
39→        val upgradedSettings = settings.toMutableMap()
40→
41→        for (toVersion in version + 1..targetVersion) {
42→            upgraders[toVersion]?.upgrade(upgradedSettings)
43→
44→            upgradeSettingsGeneric(settingsDescriptions, upgradedSettings, toVersion)
45→        }
46→
47→        return upgradedSettings
48→    }
49→
50→    private fun upgradeSettingsGeneric(
51→        settingsDescriptions: SettingsDescriptions,
52→        mutableSettings: MutableMap<String, Any?>,
53→        toVersion: Int,
54→    ) {
55→        for ((settingName, versionedSettingsDescriptions) in settingsDescriptions) {
56→            val isNewlyAddedSetting = versionedSettingsDescriptions.firstKey() == toVersion
57→            if (isNewlyAddedSetting) {
58→                val wasHandledByCustomUpgrader = mutableSettings.containsKey(settingName)
59→                if (wasHandledByCustomUpgrader) {
60→                    continue
61→                }
62→
63→                val settingDescription = versionedSettingsDescriptions[toVersion]
64→                    ?: throw AssertionError("First version of a setting must be non-null!")
65→
66→                upgradeSettingInsertDefault(mutableSettings, settingName, settingDescription)
67→            }
68→
69→            val highestVersion = versionedSettingsDescriptions.lastKey()
70→            val isRemovedSetting = highestVersion == toVersion &&
71→                versionedSettingsDescriptions[highestVersion] == null
72→
73→            if (isRemovedSetting) {
74→                mutableSettings.remove(settingName)
75→                Timber.v("Removed setting '%s'", settingName)
76→            }
77→        }
78→    }
79→
80→    private fun <T> upgradeSettingInsertDefault(
81→        mutableSettings: MutableMap<String, Any?>,
82→        settingName: String,
83→        settingDescription: SettingsDescription<T>,
84→    ) {
85→        val defaultValue = settingDescription.getDefaultValue()
86→        mutableSettings[settingName] = defaultValue
87→
88→        if (K9.isDebugLoggingEnabled) {
89→            val prettyValue = settingDescription.toPrettyString(defaultValue)
90→            Timber.v("Added new setting '%s' with default value '%s'", settingName, prettyValue)
91→        }
92→    }
93→}

 29→class AccountCreator(
 30→    private val accountColorPicker: AccountColorPicker,
 31→    private val localFoldersCreator: SpecialLocalFoldersCreator,
 32→    private val preferences: Preferences,
 33→    private val context: Context,
 34→    private val messagingController: MessagingController,
 35→    private val coroutineDispatcher: CoroutineDispatcher = Dispatchers.IO,
 36→) : AccountSetupExternalContract.AccountCreator {
 37→
 38→    @Suppress("TooGenericExceptionCaught")
 39→    override suspend fun createAccount(account: Account): AccountCreatorResult {
 40→        return try {
 41→            withContext(coroutineDispatcher) { AccountCreatorResult.Success(create(account)) }
 42→        } catch (e: Exception) {
 43→            Timber.e(e, "Error while creating new account")
 44→
 45→            AccountCreatorResult.Error(e.message ?: "Unknown create account error")
 46→        }
 47→    }
 48→
 49→    private fun create(account: Account): String {
 50→        val newAccount = preferences.newAccount(account.uuid)
 51→
 52→        newAccount.email = account.emailAddress
 53→
 54→        newAccount.setIncomingServerSettings(account.incomingServerSettings)
 55→        newAccount.outgoingServerSettings = account.outgoingServerSettings
 56→
 57→        newAccount.oAuthState = account.authorizationState
 58→
 59→        newAccount.name = account.options.accountName
 60→        newAccount.senderName = account.options.displayName
 61→        if (account.options.emailSignature != null) {
 62→            newAccount.signatureUse = true
 63→            newAccount.signature = account.options.emailSignature
 64→        }
 65→        newAccount.isNotifyNewMail = account.options.showNotification
 66→        newAccount.automaticCheckIntervalMinutes = account.options.checkFrequencyInMinutes
 67→        newAccount.displayCount = account.options.messageDisplayCount
 68→
 69→        newAccount.deletePolicy = DeletePolicyHelper.getDefaultDeletePolicy(newAccount.incomingServerSettings.type)
 70→        newAccount.chipColor = accountColorPicker.pickColor()
 71→
 72→        localFoldersCreator.createSpecialLocalFolders(newAccount)
 73→
 74→        account.specialFolderSettings?.let { specialFolderSettings ->
 75→            newAccount.setSpecialFolders(specialFolderSettings)
 76→        }
 77→
 78→        newAccount.markSetupFinished()
 79→
 80→        preferences.saveAccount(newAccount)
 81→
 82→        Core.setServicesEnabled(context)
 83→
 84→        messagingController.refreshFolderListBlocking(newAccount)
 85→
 86→        if (account.options.checkFrequencyInMinutes == -1) {
 87→            messagingController.checkMail(newAccount, false, true, false, null)
 88→        }
 89→
 90→        return newAccount.uuid
 91→    }
 92→
 93→    /**
 94→     * Set special folders by name.
 95→     *
 96→     * Since the folder list hasn't been synced yet, we don't have database IDs for the folders. So we use the same
 97→     * mechanism that is used when importing settings. See [SpecialFolderUpdater] for details.
 98→     */
 99→    private fun K9Account.setSpecialFolders(specialFolders: SpecialFolderSettings) {
100→        importedArchiveFolder = specialFolders.archiveSpecialFolderOption.toFolderServerId()
101→        archiveFolderSelection = specialFolders.archiveSpecialFolderOption.toFolderSelection()
102→
103→        importedDraftsFolder = specialFolders.draftsSpecialFolderOption.toFolderServerId()
104→        draftsFolderSelection = specialFolders.draftsSpecialFolderOption.toFolderSelection()
105→
106→        importedSentFolder = specialFolders.sentSpecialFolderOption.toFolderServerId()
107→        sentFolderSelection = specialFolders.sentSpecialFolderOption.toFolderSelection()
108→
109→        importedSpamFolder = specialFolders.spamSpecialFolderOption.toFolderServerId()
110→        spamFolderSelection = specialFolders.spamSpecialFolderOption.toFolderSelection()
111→
112→        importedTrashFolder = specialFolders.trashSpecialFolderOption.toFolderServerId()
113→        trashFolderSelection = specialFolders.trashSpecialFolderOption.toFolderSelection()
114→    }
115→
116→    private fun SpecialFolderOption.toFolderServerId(): String? {
117→        return when (this) {
118→            is SpecialFolderOption.None -> null
119→            is SpecialFolderOption.Regular -> remoteFolder.serverId.serverId
120→            is SpecialFolderOption.Special -> remoteFolder.serverId.serverId
121→        }
122→    }
123→
124→    private fun SpecialFolderOption.toFolderSelection(): SpecialFolderSelection {
125→        return when (this) {
126→            is SpecialFolderOption.None -> {
127→                if (isAutomatic) SpecialFolderSelection.AUTOMATIC else SpecialFolderSelection.MANUAL
128→            }
129→            is SpecialFolderOption.Regular -> {
130→                SpecialFolderSelection.MANUAL
131→            }
132→            is SpecialFolderOption.Special -> {
133→                if (isAutomatic) SpecialFolderSelection.AUTOMATIC else SpecialFolderSelection.MANUAL
134→            }
135→        }
136→    }
137→}

139→private fun K9Account.setIncomingServerSettings(serverSettings: ServerSettings) {
140→    if (serverSettings.type == Protocols.IMAP) {
141→        useCompression = serverSettings.isUseCompression
142→        isSendClientInfoEnabled = serverSettings.isSendClientInfo
143→        incomingServerSettings = serverSettings.copy(
144→            extra = createExtra(
145→                autoDetectNamespace = serverSettings.autoDetectNamespace,
146→                pathPrefix = serverSettings.pathPrefix,
147→            ),
148→        )
149→    } else {
150→        incomingServerSettings = serverSettings
151→    }
152→}

 12→class AccountSettingsUpgraderTest {
 13→    @Suppress("LongMethod")
 14→    @Test
 15→    fun `with combined settings upgrader`() {
 16→        val accountSettingsDescriptions = mapOf(
 17→            "notificationFolderMode" to versions(
 18→                1 to StringSetting("FIRST_CLASS"),
 19→                2 to null,
 20→            ),
 21→            "additional" to versions(
 22→                3 to StringSetting("new"),
 23→            ),
 24→        )
 25→        val folderSettingsUpgrader = FolderSettingsUpgrader(
 26→            settingsDescriptions = mapOf(
 27→                "notificationClass" to versions(
 28→                    1 to StringSetting("NO_CLASS"),
 29→                    2 to null,
 30→                ),
 31→                "notificationEnabled" to versions(
 32→                    2 to BooleanSetting(false),
 33→                ),
 34→            ),
 35→            upgraders = emptyMap(),
 36→        )
 37→        val combinedUpgraders = mapOf(
 38→            2 to {
 39→                CombinedSettingsUpgrader { account ->
 40→                    val notificationFolderMode = account.settings["notificationFolderMode"]
 41→                    account.copy(
 42→                        folders = account.folders.map { folder ->
 43→                            folder.copy(
 44→                                settings = folder.settings.toMutableMap().apply {
 45→                                    this["notificationEnabled"] = this["notificationClass"] == notificationFolderMode
 46→                                },
 47→                            )
 48→                        },
 49→                    )
 50→                }
 51→            },
 52→        )
 53→        val accountSettingsUpgrader = AccountSettingsUpgrader(
 54→            identitySettingsUpgrader = irrelevantIdentitySettingsUpgrader(),
 55→            folderSettingsUpgrader = folderSettingsUpgrader,
 56→            serverSettingsUpgrader = irrelevantServerSettingsUpgrader(),
 57→            latestVersion = 3,
 58→            settingsDescriptions = accountSettingsDescriptions,
 59→            upgraders = emptyMap(),
 60→            combinedUpgraders = combinedUpgraders,
 61→        )
 62→        val account = ValidatedSettings.Account(
 63→            uuid = "irrelevant",
 64→            name = "irrelevant",
 65→            incoming = irrelevantServer(),
 66→            outgoing = irrelevantServer(),
 67→            settings = mapOf(
 68→                "notificationFolderMode" to "SECOND_CLASS",
 69→            ),
 70→            identities = emptyList(),
 71→            folders = listOf(
 72→                ValidatedSettings.Folder(
 73→                    name = "folderOne",
 74→                    settings = mapOf(

 76→                    ),
 77→                ),
 78→                ValidatedSettings.Folder(

 80→                    settings = mapOf(
 81→                        "notificationClass" to "SECOND_CLASS",
 82→                    ),
 83→                ),
 84→            ),
 85→        )
 86→
 87→        val result = accountSettingsUpgrader.upgrade(contentVersion = 1, account)
 88→
 89→        assertThat(result).all {

 91→            prop(ValidatedSettings.Account::folders).containsExactlyInAnyOrder(
 92→                ValidatedSettings.Folder(

102→                    ),
103→                ),

41→        incomingServerPassword: String?,
42→        outgoingServerPassword: String?,

49→            account.outgoingServerSettings = account.outgoingServerSettings.newPassword(outgoingServerPassword)

215→    @Synchronized
216→    private fun loadIdentities(accountUuid: String, storage: Storage): List<Identity> {
217→        val newIdentities = ArrayList<Identity>()
218→        var ident = 0
219→        var gotOne: Boolean
220→        do {
221→            gotOne = false
222→            val name = storage.getString("$accountUuid.$IDENTITY_NAME_KEY.$ident", null)
223→            val email = storage.getString("$accountUuid.$IDENTITY_EMAIL_KEY.$ident", null)
224→            val signatureUse = storage.getBoolean("$accountUuid.signatureUse.$ident", false)
225→            val signature = storage.getString("$accountUuid.signature.$ident", null)
226→            val description = storage.getString("$accountUuid.$IDENTITY_DESCRIPTION_KEY.$ident", null)
227→            val replyTo = storage.getString("$accountUuid.replyTo.$ident", null)
228→            if (email != null) {
229→                val identity = Identity(
230→                    name = name,
231→                    email = email,
232→                    signatureUse = signatureUse,
233→                    signature = signature,
234→                    description = description,
235→                    replyTo = replyTo,
236→                )
237→                newIdentities.add(identity)
238→                gotOne = true
239→            }
240→            ident++
241→        } while (gotOne)
242→
243→        if (newIdentities.isEmpty()) {
244→            val name = storage.getString("$accountUuid.name", null)
245→            val email = storage.getString("$accountUuid.email", null)
246→            val signatureUse = storage.getBoolean("$accountUuid.signatureUse", false)
247→            val signature = storage.getString("$accountUuid.signature", null)
248→            val identity = Identity(
249→                name = name,
250→                email = email,
251→                signatureUse = signatureUse,
252→                signature = signature,
253→                description = email,
254→            )
255→            newIdentities.add(identity)
256→        }
257→
258→        return newIdentities
259→    }

263→        val accountUuid = account.uuid

376→    @Synchronized
377→    fun delete(editor: StorageEditor, storage: Storage, account: Account) {
378→        val accountUuid = account.uuid
379→
380→        // Get the list of account UUIDs
381→        val uuids =
382→            storage.getString("accountUuids", "").split(",".toRegex()).dropLastWhile { it.isEmpty() }.toTypedArray()
383→
384→        // Create a list of all account UUIDs excluding this account
385→        val newUuids = ArrayList<String>(uuids.size)
386→        for (uuid in uuids) {
387→            if (uuid != accountUuid) {
388→                newUuids.add(uuid)
389→            }
390→        }
391→
392→        // Only change the 'accountUuids' value if this account's UUID was listed before
393→        if (newUuids.size < uuids.size) {
394→            val accountUuids = Utility.combine(newUuids.toTypedArray(), ',')
395→            editor.putString("accountUuids", accountUuids)
396→        }
397→

494→    @Synchronized
495→    private fun saveIdentities(account: Account, storage: Storage, editor: StorageEditor) {
496→        deleteIdentities(account, storage, editor)
497→        var ident = 0
498→
499→        with(account) {
500→            for (identity in identities) {
501→                editor.putString("$uuid.$IDENTITY_NAME_KEY.$ident", identity.name)
502→                editor.putString("$uuid.$IDENTITY_EMAIL_KEY.$ident", identity.email)
503→                editor.putBoolean("$uuid.signatureUse.$ident", identity.signatureUse)
504→                editor.putString("$uuid.signature.$ident", identity.signature)
505→                editor.putString("$uuid.$IDENTITY_DESCRIPTION_KEY.$ident", identity.description)
506→                editor.putString("$uuid.replyTo.$ident", identity.replyTo)
507→                ident++
508→            }
509→        }
510→    }
511→
512→    @Synchronized
513→    private fun deleteIdentities(account: Account, storage: Storage, editor: StorageEditor) {
514→        val accountUuid = account.uuid
515→
516→        var identityIndex = 0
517→        var gotOne: Boolean
518→        do {
519→            gotOne = false
520→            val email = storage.getString("$accountUuid.$IDENTITY_EMAIL_KEY.$identityIndex", null)
521→            if (email != null) {
522→                editor.remove("$accountUuid.$IDENTITY_NAME_KEY.$identityIndex")
523→                editor.remove("$accountUuid.$IDENTITY_EMAIL_KEY.$identityIndex")
524→                editor.remove("$accountUuid.signatureUse.$identityIndex")
525→                editor.remove("$accountUuid.signature.$identityIndex")
526→                editor.remove("$accountUuid.$IDENTITY_DESCRIPTION_KEY.$identityIndex")
527→                editor.remove("$accountUuid.replyTo.$identityIndex")
528→                gotOne = true
529→            }
530→            identityIndex++
531→        } while (gotOne)
532→    }

534→    fun move(editor: StorageEditor, account: Account, storage: Storage, newPosition: Int) {
535→        val accountUuids = storage.getString("accountUuids", "").split(",").filter { it.isNotEmpty() }
536→        val oldPosition = accountUuids.indexOf(account.uuid)
537→        if (oldPosition == -1 || oldPosition == newPosition) return
538→
539→        val newAccountUuidsString = accountUuids.toMutableList()
540→            .apply {
541→                removeAt(oldPosition)
542→                add(newPosition, account.uuid)
543→            }
544→            .joinToString(separator = ",")
545→
546→        editor.putString("accountUuids", newAccountUuidsString)
547→    }

549→    private fun <T : Enum<T>> getEnumStringPref(storage: Storage, key: String, defaultEnum: T): T {
550→        val stringPref = storage.getString(key, null)
551→
552→        return if (stringPref == null) {
553→            defaultEnum
554→        } else {
555→            try {
556→                java.lang.Enum.valueOf<T>(defaultEnum.declaringJavaClass, stringPref)
557→            } catch (ex: IllegalArgumentException) {
558→                Timber.w(
559→                    ex,
560→                    "Unable to convert preference key [%s] value [%s] to enum of type %s",
561→                    key,
562→                    stringPref,
563→                    defaultEnum.declaringJavaClass,
564→                )
565→
566→                defaultEnum
567→            }
568→        }
569→    }

215→    @Synchronized
216→    private fun loadIdentities(accountUuid: String, storage: Storage): List<Identity> {
217→        val newIdentities = ArrayList<Identity>()
218→        var ident = 0
219→        var gotOne: Boolean
220→        do {
221→            gotOne = false
222→            val name = storage.getString("$accountUuid.$IDENTITY_NAME_KEY.$ident", null)
223→            val email = storage.getString("$accountUuid.$IDENTITY_EMAIL_KEY.$ident", null)
224→            val signatureUse = storage.getBoolean("$accountUuid.signatureUse.$ident", false)
225→            val signature = storage.getString("$accountUuid.signature.$ident", null)
226→            val description = storage.getString("$accountUuid.$IDENTITY_DESCRIPTION_KEY.$ident", null)
227→            val replyTo = storage.getString("$accountUuid.replyTo.$ident", null)
228→            if (email != null) {
229→                val identity = Identity(
230→                    name = name,
231→                    email = email,
232→                    signatureUse = signatureUse,
233→                    signature = signature,
234→                    description = description,
235→                    replyTo = replyTo,
236→                )
237→                newIdentities.add(identity)
238→                gotOne = true
239→            }
240→            ident++
241→        } while (gotOne)
242→
243→        if (newIdentities.isEmpty()) {
244→            val name = storage.getString("$accountUuid.name", null)
245→            val email = storage.getString("$accountUuid.email", null)
246→            val signatureUse = storage.getBoolean("$accountUuid.signatureUse", false)
247→            val signature = storage.getString("$accountUuid.signature", null)
248→            val identity = Identity(
249→                name = name,
250→                email = email,
251→                signatureUse = signatureUse,
252→                signature = signature,
253→                description = email,
254→            )
255→            newIdentities.add(identity)
256→        }
257→
258→        return newIdentities
259→    }

265→        if (!storage.getString("accountUuids", "").contains(account.uuid)) {

376→    @Synchronized
377→    fun delete(editor: StorageEditor, storage: Storage, account: Account) {
378→        val accountUuid = account.uuid
379→
380→        // Get the list of account UUIDs
381→        val uuids =
382→            storage.getString("accountUuids", "").split(",".toRegex()).dropLastWhile { it.isEmpty() }.toTypedArray()
383→
384→        // Create a list of all account UUIDs excluding this account
385→        val newUuids = ArrayList<String>(uuids.size)
386→        for (uuid in uuids) {
387→            if (uuid != accountUuid) {
388→                newUuids.add(uuid)
389→            }
390→        }
391→
392→        // Only change the 'accountUuids' value if this account's UUID was listed before
393→        if (newUuids.size < uuids.size) {
394→            val accountUuids = Utility.combine(newUuids.toTypedArray(), ',')

494→    @Synchronized
495→    private fun saveIdentities(account: Account, storage: Storage, editor: StorageEditor) {
496→        deleteIdentities(account, storage, editor)
497→        var ident = 0
498→
499→        with(account) {
500→            for (identity in identities) {
501→                editor.putString("$uuid.$IDENTITY_NAME_KEY.$ident", identity.name)
502→                editor.putString("$uuid.$IDENTITY_EMAIL_KEY.$ident", identity.email)
503→                editor.putBoolean("$uuid.signatureUse.$ident", identity.signatureUse)
504→                editor.putString("$uuid.signature.$ident", identity.signature)
505→                editor.putString("$uuid.$IDENTITY_DESCRIPTION_KEY.$ident", identity.description)
506→                editor.putString("$uuid.replyTo.$ident", identity.replyTo)
507→                ident++
508→            }
509→        }
510→    }
511→
512→    @Synchronized
513→    private fun deleteIdentities(account: Account, storage: Storage, editor: StorageEditor) {
514→        val accountUuid = account.uuid
515→
516→        var identityIndex = 0
517→        var gotOne: Boolean
518→        do {
519→            gotOne = false
520→            val email = storage.getString("$accountUuid.$IDENTITY_EMAIL_KEY.$identityIndex", null)
521→            if (email != null) {
522→                editor.remove("$accountUuid.$IDENTITY_NAME_KEY.$identityIndex")
523→                editor.remove("$accountUuid.$IDENTITY_EMAIL_KEY.$identityIndex")
524→                editor.remove("$accountUuid.signatureUse.$identityIndex")
525→                editor.remove("$accountUuid.signature.$identityIndex")
526→                editor.remove("$accountUuid.$IDENTITY_DESCRIPTION_KEY.$identityIndex")
527→                editor.remove("$accountUuid.replyTo.$identityIndex")
528→                gotOne = true
529→            }
530→            identityIndex++
531→        } while (gotOne)
532→    }

534→    fun move(editor: StorageEditor, account: Account, storage: Storage, newPosition: Int) {
535→        val accountUuids = storage.getString("accountUuids", "").split(",").filter { it.isNotEmpty() }
536→        val oldPosition = accountUuids.indexOf(account.uuid)
537→        if (oldPosition == -1 || oldPosition == newPosition) return
538→
539→        val newAccountUuidsString = accountUuids.toMutableList()
540→            .apply {
541→                removeAt(oldPosition)
542→                add(newPosition, account.uuid)
543→            }
544→            .joinToString(separator = ",")
545→
546→        editor.putString("accountUuids", newAccountUuidsString)
547→    }

549→    private fun <T : Enum<T>> getEnumStringPref(storage: Storage, key: String, defaultEnum: T): T {
550→        val stringPref = storage.getString(key, null)
551→
552→        return if (stringPref == null) {
553→            defaultEnum
554→        } else {
555→            try {
556→                java.lang.Enum.valueOf<T>(defaultEnum.declaringJavaClass, stringPref)
557→            } catch (ex: IllegalArgumentException) {
558→                Timber.w(
559→                    ex,
560→                    "Unable to convert preference key [%s] value [%s] to enum of type %s",
561→                    key,
562→                    stringPref,
563→                    defaultEnum.declaringJavaClass,
564→                )
565→
566→                defaultEnum
567→            }
568→        }
569→    }
